description: Fix the version mismatch bug caused by stale `^0.0.100` internal package dependencies that should be `*` (replaced by the publish workflow), fix the EADDRINUSE crash that shows a raw Node.js stack trace, and suppress the spurious 'Unknown config key telemetryNoticeShown' log warning.

userStories.title: Fix stale ^0.0.100 internal package deps to use * placeholder
userStories.description: All platform packages use `^0.0.100` for their @opentabs-dev/ internal dependencies instead of `*`. The publish workflow (`.github/workflows/publish-platform.yml`) only replaces `*` with `^$VERSION` at publish time. Because `^0.0.100` for `0.0.x` semver is pinned exactly to 0.0.100, users who install `@opentabs-dev/cli@0.0.102` get `@opentabs-dev/mcp-server@0.0.100`, causing version mismatch warnings on every start, extension downgrade noise, and circular doctor advice. Change all internal @opentabs-dev/ dependency values from `^0.0.100` to `*` in the affected package.json files.
acceptanceCriteria: No other versioned @opentabs-dev/ deps (like ^0.0.x) remain in any platform package.json
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/package.json (change 5 internal deps)
- platform/mcp-server/package.json (change 1 internal dep)
- platform/browser-extension/package.json (change 1 internal dep)
- platform/plugin-sdk/package.json (change 1 internal dep)
- platform/plugin-tools/package.json (change 2 internal deps)
- platform/create-plugin/package.json (change 1 internal dep)

Context:
The publish workflow at `.github/workflows/publish-platform.yml` lines 85-91 replaces `*` with `^$VERSION` at publish time:
```javascript
for (const [name, ver] of Object.entries(depMap)) {
  if (name.startsWith('@opentabs-dev/') && ver === '*') {
    depMap[name] = '^$VERSION';
  }
}
```
It ONLY replaces `*`, so stale `^0.0.100` values are never updated. In npm semver, `^0.0.100` means EXACTLY 0.0.100 (locked) for 0.0.x versions — so `@opentabs-dev/cli@0.0.102` installs `@opentabs-dev/mcp-server@0.0.100` even though 0.0.102 exists. This causes immediate version mismatch warnings and cascading extension version issues.

Changes:
In each package.json file, change every occurrence of `"^0.0.100"` for `@opentabs-dev/` scoped packages to `"*"`. Only change INTERNAL @opentabs-dev/ packages — do NOT change external dependencies.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any non-@opentabs-dev/ dependency versions
- Do NOT change the `"version"` field of each package itself — only change dependency values
- After the change, run `npm install` so the lockfile updates (npm will resolve `*` to the locally built packages in the monorepo workspace)

userStories.title: Fix EADDRINUSE crash — exit cleanly instead of throwing
userStories.description: When the MCP server tries to bind a port that's already in use, `handleListenError` correctly logs a helpful message but then calls `throw error`, causing an unhandled promise rejection with a raw Node.js stack trace printed to stderr (and captured in server.log). Change the EADDRINUSE case to call `process.exit(1)` instead of re-throwing, so the process exits cleanly after printing the helpful message.
acceptanceCriteria: In handleListenError, when error.code === 'EADDRINUSE', the function calls process.exit(1) after logging the helpful message rather than throwing
The non-EADDRINUSE path in handleListenError still throws (for unexpected listen errors)
Running `opentabs start` when the port is already in use shows the helpful message and exits without a raw Node.js stack trace

userStories.title: Add missing config keys to KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS to suppress log warnings
userStories.description: The MCP server's `parseConfigRecord` warns 'Unknown config key X in config.json' for any key not in KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS. The `telemetryNoticeShown` key (written by older CLI versions) and `telemetry` (written by `opentabs telemetry disable`) are both absent from this set, causing spurious warnings in server.log for every new user. Add both keys to KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS.
acceptanceCriteria: Starting the server with a config.json containing telemetryNoticeShown produces no 'Unknown config key' warning in the logs
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts (KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS set)

Context:
At lines 163-171 in platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts:
```typescript
const KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS = new Set([
  'version',
  'localPlugins',
  'localPluginDirs',
  'permissions',
  'settings',
  'additionalAllowedDirectories',
  'updateCheckIntervalMinutes',
]);
```
Both `telemetryNoticeShown` (written by older CLI to track first-run notice) and `telemetry` (written by `opentabs telemetry disable` — sets it to `false`) are absent. When `parseConfigRecord` (lines 209-224) iterates config keys and finds these, it logs a warning.

Changes:
Add both keys to KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS:
```typescript
const KNOWN_CONFIG_KEYS = new Set([
  'version',
  'localPlugins',
  'localPluginDirs',
  'permissions',
  'settings',
  'additionalAllowedDirectories',
  'updateCheckIntervalMinutes',
  'telemetryNoticeShown',  // legacy key from older CLI versions
  'telemetry',             // opt-out flag written by `opentabs telemetry disable`
]);
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other logic in parseConfigRecord
- Do NOT add migration to remove telemetryNoticeShown — just suppress the warning
